summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring ceremonial swearing-in of three new police officers and promotion of a detective to sergeant, as well as life-saving awards for staff involved in two cardiac arrest incidents. The board approved the 2024 budget, a cemetery mowing contract, a used V-box purchase for snow removal, initiated the process for a criteria architect for the demolition and reconstruction of Fire Station 18, passed a resolution of necessity to replace an expiring five-year Fire/EMS operating levy at the same millage, and processed grant-related financial motions for the Brownfield Grant, ARPA Retention Grant, and Tech Cred Drone Training Grant.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for October 24 through November 13, 2023
Approved agreement with Mullins Maintenance for 2024 cemetery mowing
Approved purchase of used Buyer's two-ton stainless V-box from Walker Landscape not to exceed $6,000
Approved refund of $216 to Christy Hodge for duplicate cemetery foundation payment
Approved motion to solicit statement of qualifications for criteria architect/engineer for demolition of Station 18 and construction of new fire station
Approved increase of revenue of $267,056.85 from Brownfield Grant
Approved increase of appropriations of $267,056.85 for Brownfield Grant
Approved payment of $267,056.85 to Patriot Engineering from Brownfield Grant funds
Approved increase of revenue of $176,671.38 from ARPA Retention Grant
Approved increase of appropriations of $176,671.38 from ARPA Retention Grant
Approved increase of appropriations of $7,800 from Tech Cred Drone Training Grant
Approved Resolution of Necessity R0222023 to replace expiring five-year Fire/EMS operating levy at same millage
Approved motion for executive session under ORC 121.22 G1 and G4
Approved motion to adjourn at 8:48 PM
action_items: Chief Myers to bring motion to proceed on levy replacement at next meeting and submit documents to Board of Elections before December 20
Administrator Rhoads to complete revenue true-up report in December and present to board
Administrator Rhoads to audit lighting district in 2024 to address expenses exceeding revenue
Administrator Rhoads to come back with recommendations on encumbering ARPA funds before end of 2024
Director Meadows to attend Southwest Public Works Association meeting and follow up on cape seal/alternative paving options for 2025
HR Manager Eichenhorst to finalize healthcare renewal after meeting the following day
Jen Foster to establish wellness committee with representatives from each department meeting monthly
Laura Englund and Jen to implement interest savings account by January
Administrator Rhoads to earmark $75,000 for road paving in 2025 and seek grant match for $300,000 total
key_amounts: $6,000 - purchase of used Buyer's two-ton stainless V-box for snow removal
$216 - refund to Christy Hodge for duplicate cemetery foundation payment
$267,056.85 - Brownfield Grant revenue/payment to Patriot Engineering
$387,700 - total Brownfield Grant amount from State of Ohio
$176,671.38 - ARPA Retention Grant for police and fire ($4,000 per officer/firefighter)
$7,800 - Tech Cred Drone Training Grant appropriation
$141.28/year - current levy cost for $100,000 home; $16 annual increase with replacement
$3.4 million - estimated starting unencumbered balance across all funds for 2024
$900,000 - road paving accomplished in prior year
$120,000 - two new police cruisers budgeted for 2024
$329,000 - quoted cost for new medic unit (up from $250,000 in 2020)
$60,000 - estimated cape seal cost for O'Banion Meadows subdivision vs $90,000-$100,000 for mill and fill
$30,000 - conservative estimate for interest savings account revenue
$75,000 - earmarked for road paving toward 2025 grant match
notable_topics: Swearing-in of three new police officers (Lunsford, Sickles, Pangallo)
Promotion of Chris McMillan to police sergeant
Life-saving awards for Chad Meadows (youth football cardiac arrest) and Sergeant Skates/medics (Brian Hammonds cardiac arrest)
Fire Station 18 demolition and new construction process initiation
Five-year Fire/EMS operating levy replacement (Resolution of Necessity R0222023)
2024 budget presentation and approval across all departments
Cemetery mowing contract awarded to Mullins Maintenance
Brownfield Grant payments for Combs Dumps environmental assessment
ARPA first responder retention grant distribution
Alternative road paving methods (cape seal vs mill and fill)
attendees: Wendy (Chris McMillan's girlfriend, Clermont County deputy)
Mike Ford (Chris McMillan's father, retired Union Township police sergeant)
notes: The meeting featured an extended ceremonial portion with an intermission for refreshments provided by the CPAAA (Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association). A drone purchased by CPAAA was actively being used during the meeting for a critical missing person search. The board entered executive session at 8:17 PM regarding employee compensation/negotiations under ORC 121.22 G1 and G4, returning at 8:48 PM with no action taken. Roll call responses during the cemetery mowing vote transcript appear garbled with extra trustee names mentioned that are not actual board members. Chief Myers noted medic unit delivery could be delayed to 2027 due to UAW strike impacts.
title: BOT 11-14-23
